He does drop one quiz however. Overall, wouldn't take him again unless you are very, very, very passionate and time devoted to this class.

Professor Ahmed is a really good professor. He gives a lot of really long and complex codes to write for homework. But after writing the code, you'd be an expert at the topic he is teaching. It's not an easy A class. But it's a class that will prepare you in every way possible for all other future classes. He gives a good challenge for his students

Professor Ahmed is a nice man and gives good lectures. That being said he goes overtime 10 min on avg. Lots of homework that are sometimes really complicated for an intro class. Quizzes make you look for the tiniest of things like a semicolon out of place that ends a loop. Not really fun for a new learner who hasn't been coding long. I'm tired.

I'm p new to programming and came to see if I was the only one sweating. He wants us to truly understand C, the exams/quiz can be difficult but I feel that I am gaining a lot from the course. With that said, the midterm tanked my grade and I'm practicing a ton to do great on the final. I hope that practice is what it takes to pass >.<

For a course that is "introduction to programming with c", this class is extremely hard, even with a slight background in programming. I have friends who have worked with C for years that help tutor me to understand the work better, and sometimes even they get stumped by the programming assignments. Currently have a B, but not for long.

A terrible professor aided by even worse TAs. The tests and quizzes are written in broken English and contain errors that make getting to the right answer borderline impossible. You could be very proficient in the C coding language (demonstrated by excellent grades in the lab) but fail the course because of the poor delivery.

This professor is a nice guy, but the class was structured in such a challenging way, especially for someone who knows nothing about C. The exams (called quizzes but take up 15% of your grade) only have 4-5 questions, making it really hard to have room for error. I have also reached out to multiple tutors and the professor. Dropping the class now.

Great professor. Goes over class time by 15 minutes every single class which gets frustrating when you have multiple classes every day. But he explains everything pretty well and is willing to help when you ask for it. MAKE SURE YOU PRACTICE ON YOUR OWN. You need to put forth some effort yourself, given the nature of the class itself.

I was initially skeptical about professor Ahmed and wasn't sure how I felt about him. However, as the semester progressed, he shows that he really cares about his students and will do his best to accommodate you. This class is difficult, but it's all to prepare you for the FE.

He is overall a very nice professor and can be generous with his grading at times. But CS1 is a difficult class and requires a lot of work. His quizzes are like tests so make sure to study for them like you would an exam. Make sure you attend office hours regularly to get help assignments and any other topics you may struggle with.

I took him a couple years ago. He is a very kind teacher. There were a bunch of people cheating on quiz but he gave them a chance to fix themselves by writing an apology letter. He has a bit of accent but you will get used to it. I would take him again.

A great professor. Assignments would take a lot longer than you expected and instructions sometimes weren't very clear, but gave a lot of extra credit and he definitely cares about the success of his students.

Prof. Ahmed was a great choice! He went over everything in detail (but not too much detail) and made sure we knew what we were doing. He'd take time to answer questions and would adjust the course schedule as necessary. He often extended deadlines and gave extra credit to those who submitted by the original deadline. Overall great professor!

The online lectures went on longer than they were supposed to sometimes. However the lectures are very informative and helpful. There are also opportunities for extra credit. If you're a first time ever coder like me I would take advantage of it. The class wasn't easy but if you pay attention it's possible to get a very good grade.

Dr. Ahmed cares a lot for his students and he is very understanding. He tries to be fair to the students and will do his best to accommodate you if you have a concern. Yes, this class is very difficult, but it is meant to be so you can pass the FE.

Everybody complaining about this professor yall are capping right now. During lectures has a set PowerPoint but spends good chunk coding during lectures, running through different possible scenarios. Everything is fair game as long as you do all his exercises/coding problems the tests are fine. End of the semester offered lots of extra credit.

Never actually met this man but I love him, amazing professor and really cares for his students and their success. He sometimes gets carried away with his lectures, and if he's writing by hand you wont know what it says, but you can ask anything and he's happy to help. His office hours are extremely helpful. Great to take w/o coding experience.

Actual rating 4.5. Homework assignments can pile up, lectures are pretty helpful (codes during lecture about half of time) , tests are fair, but difficult. This isn't an easy A, but this class is for CS1, so I'm not sure you want it *easy*. Email responses /announcements are usually later in the day (asking questions at night gets quicker response)

Y'all ain't studying enough. This man responds to your emails in 5 minutes at midnight with any question you might have. His lectures are clearly organized and the PowerPoints do the best job they can to explain the material. Quizzes and Tests are filled with trick questions, but if you study, you'll catch these. Great Prep for CS1.

I am an Engineering major and I needed to take Intro to C. Since I knew programming I was saved and I got an A. Initially, it is an easy class but after the mid-term, it really started becoming hard and pandemic made it even harder. His lectures are not good and you have to give your full attention to understand it because of his accent.

Tests consist of only trick questions, he will only let you talk to the TAs, Goes on insane rants during lectures with random scribbling and tells you math rather then how to code said math. 2 long coding assignments every week that will keep you up for days. Supposed to be an intro to coding class but has assignments with 150+ lines of code. DON'T

literally do NOT take him if you do not have any prior coding knowledge. you're given really no help unless you go to tutoring sessions, and even then it could still be confusing. lectures aren't much of a help as he reads only off slides. assignments/quizzes/exams are generally a bit too much for an intro course

His standards are through the roof, and yet his class is a fiasco. I have a BS already, and I've never had a professor with a ten way grade split and a grade offset. class was between 4 platforms. there are so many different types of projects that you can't keep track. Guess what? Neither can he. That's why he has 10-15 TA's, tutors, etc. Wow.

Prof. Ahmed knows what he's teaching, but not how to teach others. He spends most of lecture speeding through slides and scribbling indecipherable drawings. Any time the class was lost, he told them to figure it out on their own. The assignments are way more in-depth than the examples he covers. Have a question? He'll defer you to a TA every time.

He truly is passionate about his students/what he teaches, but forgets we are stressed during a pandemic. He assigns too much hw for an intro course and Ive had pros tutor me that dont even know how to solve them. Quizzes/tests are full of trick questions,regardless of how well you know the content. I got -10pts for forgetting a space in my code.

I passed the CS foundation exam after taking his class. I passed it on my first attempt. Some of his practice problems are from old FE exams! Plus, he bases off of Guha's class. Yes, you have to make time outside of class to read, code, and understand the concepts. This is a hard class, and it will be hard no matter which professor you take.
Professor Dr. Ahmed is a fantastic professor, but he moves fast right off the base. I just didn't need that, also, make sure you have a webcam (during the COVID-19 pandemic) for online classes.

He is medium difficulty but couldn't recommend if you need to take cs2. He doesn't actually hard code in the class, more shows the code and explains the concepts. His tests and quizzes are usually: write the code for the function, what's wrong with the code, what the code output is. Be prepared for lab midterm/final along with regular midterm/final

The amount of material that was covered in this class was insane, and the professor only gave us mild guidance of what to expect on exams. Over the summer, we had 100 people withdraw out of 250, so don't fall behind early. The TA's graded everything really harsh, and even made mistakes in grading. Final was 26 questions, and had no time to finish.

This is a programming class, hence it's meant to be hard. But if you attend all lectures and practice the work, it'll be easier. Professor A cares about his students and is available outside of class. He provides external sources to help with the class. In his lectures, he really takes the time to explain each topic and will answer questions.

Ahmed certainly didn't take it easy on us considering recent events. There were constant assignments with stringent requirements, exercises, and time crunch "quizzes" and exams. He did drop an assignment although it only really seemed to benefit those that did poorly on them, not really fair in my case. Advice, just be prepared to constantly work.

Prof Ahmed knows this class can be hard for some people and the class is structured to help you pass. Just focus on studying for the exams and the code he provides really helps. The TAs I had were really tough in grading assignments and almost cost me passing the class so that tag might not apply.

Professor Ahmed is a great professor. I would say that his grading is more than fair and he managed his class extremely well given the circumstances of online teaching due to the pandemic. He is willing to help you! Just reach out and send an email. Lectures might be slow, but he makes sure to get all the content across.

I loved being in Professor Ahmed's class. Although this is an intro class, I did come in with prior knowledge. After the midterm, you really have to strap in. My notes were enough to complete the homework. He cares about his student and is responsive when you have questions. More time on the quizzes would have been nice. I always ran out of time.

Professor Ahmed was an absolute gem! Despite having an accent, his pronunciation is clear and his lectures are easy to follow. He codes along side you during lecture and asks questions which keeps everyone engaged. This class is hard but that's not his fault. He will however do everything he can to help and teach you everything you need for CS1.

For an intro class, I think he does a good job teaching the material, but that is not enough to ignore his other mistakes. He does not accommodate students that work, he gives tests/quizzes on non-class days. His quizzes and tests are much harder than homework and lectures. He intentionally uses confusing or misleading logic in his problems.
this professor is a joke. He makes no effort to accommodate students at all

Take this professor if you have a few years of coding experience. If you have never coded before and expect this class to make sense, chose another professor. Although this is an intro class, any assesment (quizzes, tests, labs) are immensely more difficult than taught in lectures. Avoid this professor if you need to pass, he expects expertise.

hes a good CS1 professor. I had him, and the work you put in greatly reflects your grade. and if you have a grade to dispute, or want to fix your grade, he will go great lengths to help you and get the grade you deserve, plus his grading scale is more forgiving than people let on. between him and Meade, take him.

First off- if you skip lecture, you will not pass. Lectures are very in-depth and he walks you through complicated code. He knows a lot and his projects and assignments help prepare you for the Foundation Exam. A lot of his quizzes, assignments, and projects are taken straight from past foundation exams. Don't be afraid to ask questions or email.

I discovered after the drop date had passed that I no longer wanted to be a CS major. Getting the grade I did in this class took every last brain cell I had and then some. Even if you are truly passionate about CS, this class is stressful and can be very difficult. Great guy, but as a professor very strenuous, even during the COVID mess.

His grading scale is headache inducing and meant to flunk you. He takes pride in making the class difficult. When in reality all it does is increase cheating. What a goofy. 70% of your grade is comprised of tests which are on pencil and paper that you will have to write code on. Sickening . Glad it's over with. People have careers to reach.

Teaches us like we're CS majors. Made sense until after midterms. Gives lab assignments that are ridiculously more advanced than what he does in class, and the final project topped that! Gives your 25 mins to write codes that'll take 45mins in quizzes, AT LEAST. He cares about students, but can't teach at an intro level. Take this class online!

Very caring professor who wants to make sure you pass the class and learn. Always replied to emails ASAP, listens to students complaints and changes things as needed, curves at the end. Tests and quizzes are a little difficult but make sure that you study for them and you'll be fine. I'm an IT major and passed with an A-!
Good professor! Material is easy to follow at first but is challenging later in the term. He's not there to watch his student fail.

His accent is a bit difficult to understand but the lecture notes are online. Keep up with the powerpoints, ask for her when needed, and never skip a lab class! Labs are an easy grade. Quizzes are not too bad as long as you study.

I understand a lot of people are not a fan of this professor. Yes, the tests are difficult but it's a computer science class. A 66 in his class is a C (passing) so obviously things are a bit tough but as long as you want to pass and turn in all your assignments the class shouldn't be too stressful. Attend the labs too! Caring professor and helpful!

Honestly, the class was a bit difficult but his grading scale was very lenient. Professor Ahmed moves grades around to help out his students, is understanding, and will find ways to help out as much as he can. He's not the type of teacher to want to see his students fail. As long as you put in the work you will pass the class!
He will give you his soul if you want to learn and if you need help on the studying.. Give good materials. Do not take if you just want an easy ride and pass.
DO NOT TAKE!! His grading scale is ridiculous. You will literally only pass if you are a programming god and are good a taking test. Dont waste your money choose another professor.

Terrible professor, cant spell anything especially on the tests, gives unclear instructions on tests & expects intro students to be programming gods. its not the hardest to follow at the beginning but a quarter through the semester & you will get lost quick! Avrgs on tests were always Ds and below. The grading is absolutely terrible
Dont take this class, especially with him. He is an awful teacher who does not seem to contain any form of common sense and is a total incompetent.

The grading scale enough is a reason for a 0/5. This class is a weed out class no matter who teaches it. He is clearly trying to fail people with his passive aggressive comments and grading scale. There is no way the final should be worth 49% of your grade. And the other 20% are two other exams. His accent is so thick you have to read on your own.

Great Professor! One of the best professors I have ever had. Regardless of who you take this course with, it requires effort. I came into this class feeling programming was not my thing and was thinking about changing majors and then ended up sticking to my major because of his class. This professor is great, he responds literally anytime day/night

Dr. Ahmed explains concepts clearly. The class gets much harder throughout the year so be prepared to put in the work. He is a nice guy and will gladly help students outside of class. Very respectful and professional towards his students.

CS weed out class. Explains very clearly. Need good C programming background. Otherwise, many coding concepts will go over your head. Extra credit and lenient grader. Questions are fair based on the materials. Homework are very helpful for practice. You need to study. No easy ride. Enjoying his lecture even in online. Big syllabus, so lot homework

He is a nice guy but this class is a pain. His lectures are hard to understand and he goes over material very quickly. He gives about 20 minutes to finish quizzes that I would say at least need 45 minutes. Exams are a lot harder than the exercises given to practice. Also, the Exams are worth half your grade so good luck. No extra credit either.

Dr. Ahmed tries his best, but did not have a good rep with my peers in this class. I thought his lectures were clear and engaging. His quizzes clear and moderately difficult. His projects were not off the charts difficult either. Have to put in the work but he is a nice guy, and certainly better than the other CS teachers at UCF in my opinion.

As kind and accommodating he tries to be, the class was very difficult. I had never programmed before and though the lectures are decent, they aren't enough to excel the many exams/quizzes, exercises, and labs he assigns. However, he seems to be one of the better choices in the CS department.

printf("Nice guy, knows C language well. Course material is slides and are somewhat confusing and disorganized. Lectures well, but accent is thick. Quizzes/Exams are too hard, timed too short and weighted too high. Coding starts easy, gets long/complex. Labs are ok. TA accessibility low. High withdraw rate. You must be all in to pass.");

He's a very experienced coder but as an intro class, it is EXTREMELY difficult to grasp concepts if you've never coded before. The tests are. extremely difficult and you get little to no time to read the questions let alone write an answer. He thinks everyone is an experienced coder and proficient in C when kids are clearly struggling.
He is so caring and respectful. Only reason students give him bad rating is for his accent which is not his fault. but he is a good professor.

Solid prof. Language barrier isn't too bad but there are instances where he will say something in a way that is confusing. Knows his subject well, and is good at conveying not just methodology but concepts of different C subjects. YOU NEED TO ATTEND LECTURES AND DO HW. If you don't you will be left behind. He makes this very clear in first lecture.

Very inconsistent in everything he does. Grading and organization is all over the place. Also his very choppy english can sometimes make directions very hard to understand. This is definently not an intro class and you will work for every point in this class. The homework is significantly harder then the material he teaches in lectures.

This is NOT an intro class. Don't take this class if you don't already know how to code. I bought several outside resources in order to survive this class and his homework assignments are significantly harder than what he teaches in the lecture. He is a nice guy but does't know how to simplify the information for someone who has not coded.

I normally dont write review, but damn this class is a mess. Like others have stated you are bombarded with powerpoints and pdfs almost daily. Homeworks mixed up with projects, labs and exams. This crap is non stop every week (except exams of course). Then theres the terrible scheduling of having lab exam followed by the class exam!

He is very friendly and Very helpful in email and office. I really like his lecture. He shows the concepts steps by steps and then show the coding that really makes sense. His homework is really a good practice for the exam. Make sure you really do homework and understand them to pass the exam. Exams are conceptual and not memorization in general.

Ill start with AVOID! This class is a fcking disaster. He crams homeworks, projects, quizzes, powerpoints all at once. Get ready to get hw uploaded on weekends at 2am. Instead of learning at proper pace, you have to rush through all the busywork for the sake finishing. He over complicates topics for no reason and has weird wording.

Get ready to teach yourself. He is the most useless teacher I have ever had the displeasure of having. He was so disorganized, unprofessional and scattered I was amazed that he still had a job by the end of the semester. He showed up to a test late, with the wrong test. He skipped classes with no warning. For your GPA and sanity, stay away!

Took the exam today. It was very fair. Not too easy. Also not too hard if you study his materials. The whole process prepared me for the exam. People who say he only ask you to write code in paper have no idea that it is very common. Also in tech interview, you have to write code on board. He also gave many Computer coding in lab and assignments.

This class is a disaster. Theres Homework, projects, quizzes and exams all due at the same time. You can never catch a break to actually study because you have to rush to meet his ridiculous deadlines. The average of the class shows that when currently people are on D and below. Worst of all we code on PAPER! Incredibly tedious and easy to screw up

took him Fall 2019, he was perfect His lecture are based on PowerPoint slides that he got from other teachers, explains topics very well. Exercises are super important for the quizzes 6 of them 2 aredropped but also important for foundation exam as questions on them are from previous exams,he go everything on Foundation, so prepared I got a 98
This isnt a professor you take if you aren't a pro at coding. If you're new to coding then you'll fail this class

Super thick accent, speaks extremely quietly, makes you do all your quizzes and tests on paper with no compiler. I feel like if I didn't already have prior coding knowledge I wouldn't have passed this class. He also only has late-night classes, I have to take him again for cs1. I'm only picking him because my only other choice has terrible ratings

His PowerPoints are useful, his assignments have clear criteria, and he is the kindest professor I've had in my three years at UCF. He extends assignments if they are difficult and provides lots of extra credit. The class itself will be hard no matter who teaches it, but Dr. Ahmed's was not difficult to succeed at, as long as you read the slides.

Decent professor. Makes you WORK HARD. Lecture slides are useful with egs problems. Do all programming assignments and exercises. Quizzes are like nuclear bombs. Tests were fair. You cannot be lazy, go to TA office hours, practise and study. Gets difficult in the end when he starts teaching structures and File I/O. So get ready.

Dr Ahmed cares about his students. Exercises, assignments, lecture powepoints,, and uploaded code prepares u for quizzes, midterm, final. I say he's a great professor through and through, but the last lab quiz pissed me off, and there was a student basically yelling at him after it

Good lecture notes with examples. Each word is important for tests. Make you work hard. I am new in programming and now I feel confident about my C skills. You have to be passionate to learn. Otherwise you might get lost. This course is not just to attend and memorize. He is very helpful in email and office.

He's very nice and willing to help, and he provides good material while being a solid lecturer. I would say there are too many assignments overall leading to students just "getting it done" without learning from them. Exams consist of problem solving rather than memorization (good) but there isn't enough time to think through a solution (bad).

Take my review with a grain of salt, since between Dorian and a football game, we've missed 4 classes. We're fairly behind the other lecture and as a CS student preparing for FE, it's kinda unnerving. Meade's class is a few weeks ahead of us. Ahmed is a fine lecturer if you can get past the accent, but his class feels more geared towards non-CS ppl

It's his first year teaching Intro to C so I understand why he's not organized yet, but man is it rough. Even if you do all of the assignments his quizzes are still hard because he words them oddly or makes them confusing. The professor himself is a good guy, but if you can take someone else I would. At least until he gets settled in.
Hella homework, very difficult quizzes even if you know the programming, I learn more from his powerpoints that he uploads than his lectures, but he an aight dude.

New professor so the course was very confusing. He is a really nice guy but strict about miscellaneous things. He will learn and get better. Grading criteria is super confusing but you should do well with little effort.

Easy class. Just studying the slides are enough. If you can remember stuff from slides for quizzes and tests, you will get an A. Attendance points. Man is a sweetheart. He is very kind and caring. Easy tests and chill class.

Ahmed is a very nice person/ ok teacher. His assignments had lots of typos and unclear instructions, but weren't very hard. First semester teaching here. She struggled a lot with the length of the class so some topics were not explained with enough details, which made the class harder. Homework and exams were hard but fair (easier than CS1). On another note, mandatory attendance for labs was awful (10% of grade).

I DO NOT recommend her. Her final felt unfair as it's only 60 mins for 47 questions including 5 long written response code. I studied so much for this course, giving it many hours. If you want a chill and easier side of a professor don't take her! Also she'll catch you with AI,I didn't even use it for advice or examples, couldn't risk getting a 0..

She is very kind and clearly cares about her students. She moves quickly through topics and expects a lot of outside studying. The workload is heavy with frequent quizzes, exams, and homework. However, she curves generously since averages are usually very low. Super hard class, but I'd recommend her and would take her again.

As a non cs student, I was worried about this class but Dr Ali made it easy to pass. Just watch the lectures and use them to write your codes. Start the assignments when they're posted so you have time to ask questions if needed. Exams were hard but doable.

She has the most important aptitude that a professor should have... communication. Great communication. The best I've ever had. But everyone knows communication sometimes doesnt cut it, which is why this other quality makes it perfect. She is extremely caring. She wants you to succeed, and she doesn't leave anyone behind. If you can take her, do.

- Be aware of your own risk this professor doesn't care of your grade and don't teach you half of the material and her lectures is really pointless if you wanna pass she told numerous of students that they complain and she has an bad attitude as well .

Said the final would be similar to the earlier exams, then gave us a 47-question test in 60 minutes, which is more questions and less time than the first two. Assigned homework over Thanksgiving break and didn't explain what to expect on the final until 2 days before finals. I'd call it poorly organized, but there wasn't any to begin with.

Awesome professor. Super nice and really helpful during office hours. Definitely cares about students. Live coding and explains well (sometimes gets a little lost but if you pay attention you'll be fine). CS1 is a hard class but she simplified the topics and gave tons of practice. 10/10.

I have never done a Rate My Professor before, but she is a genuinely awful professor. Did you add a comment in your code? Oh that's AI and no way you did that. Talk to your peer for help on assignment questions? Get 50% Here is your exam review. Take the exam and notice how it is nothing like it. Bro Code teaches better in 5-10 minutes.
Just do not take CS2 with her!!!!

- Genuinely good hearted professor
- Trying to use AI Detection system that is flagging innocent students
- Students are now putting more effort into preparing for validation checks proving they didn't cheat than actually doing assignments and learning.
If you try in her class you will get a zero for "using AI."
Great professor, was her first time and she did great. Her explanations were sometimes not the best but she really tries to help her students. Very sweet and kind person.

Dr. Ali is honestly one of the best CS professors I've had so far. She really cares about her students and them succeeding in her class. Her exams are fair just go over the practice problems she gives out and you will be just fine. She also explains things very well at her office hours and is very generous with partial credit on her exams.
Teacher was good at her job. I personally didn't go to class but the class was fairly okay to get by as long as you reviewed enough. Much less stressful than the other CS1 teachers.

professor simply can't code and honestly seems lost. she's nice, but knows so little that every time i asked something she just went, “i'm not sure.” her exam review was from another prof and had nothing to do with her actual tests, so i had no clue what'd be on it. got a B+ but wouldn't take her again. Instead, take Ahmed, Steinberg, or Gerber.

Ali is an amazing professor and cares about her students. Great lectures with plenty of examples conceptual AND coding. There are programming assignments that align great with what we are learning as well as quizzes that are multiple tries. She does a lot of practice from the FE and emphasizes preparing us for it. I learned so much in this class!

Dr Ali is very kind and caring Professor who wants you to succeed. Her tests are super chill and fair which is a breath of fresh air for the students that aren't in CS but still prepares CS students well for the FE.

Review is for COT4210. I have never written a review on here for any professor but I had to go out of my way this time for Dr. Ali. 
Although it was her first time ever teaching this course, she made a huge effort in making sure she was conveying the correct material and making sure we understood everything. Her lectures were also recorded! Amazing

I was worried about failing this class as I'm mediocre at coding, but Dr. Ali is truly the best. She really cares and wants you to pass, and is genuinely one of the kindest, coolest professors I've ever had at UCF. I'd highly recommend her for this class and any other class she teaches. She is the epitome of what a CS professor should strive to be!

Dr. Ali was so thoughtful and caring this whole semester. She responds so quickly to emails and is genuinely kind. her lectures are pretty good and she explains the concepts in CS1 well. Her lectures were recorded as well. I would definitely have her as a professor again if given the chance

Ali is a very good professor; CS1 is a difficult class regardless but Ali is one of the best options you can take to pass the class. I was expecting to leave this class with a C at the start of the semester but I ended up getting an A.

Dr. Ali is a wonderful professor, and did an amazing job for her first semester teaching. She gave good lectures and resources, listened to and implemented feedback from her students (like recording lectures), and overall is a kind and caring person. Exams can be tough, but she was generous with curves. Overall, she's an excellent professor.

This is my 3rd time taking CS1 and let me say Dr. Ali has been the most wonderful professor I have had. She truly takes her time to make sure you understand and truly wants you to pass. Her lectures are easy to understand and her exams are very similar to the study guide. I feel so prepared for the FE all thanks to Dr. Ali.

Given that this was her first semester as a professor, she was wonderful. Extremely caring and respectful, she put a lot of effort into the class and really wanted us to learn and succeed. Some of the concepts were difficult to understand and she wasn't always amazing at explaining them, but I imagine she will get better with experience.

Dr Ali was a really good teacher for it being her first semester. She really cares about the success of her students. The assignments we're fair with enough time to complete them and focused mainly on the topics she was teaching at the time. Her exams we're fair and focused more on concepts. She was also really helpful during office hours.

She's a great professor. I feel like I'm prepared for the FE, and she gave us extra credit on the exams related to the syllabus! I'd love to have her class again, and if you're debating on taking her, go for it! She curved exams if the average was lower than expected, and recorded lectures so we could revise at any time.

The Best Professor I ever had at UCF! Professor Ali is what all professors should strive to be. She is nothing but caring about her students and so kind. Even though CS1 is a terrifying class for many STEM majors, she made it seem less daunting. I hope to have other classes with her. 100/10.

As a relatively new professor, Dr. Ali blew away my expectations as a Computer Science professor. While she has had a few bumps across the road, she provides a ton of resources, such as C files she writes in lectures and the solutions for assignments. You are graded based on assignments, quizzes, and exams, and the lowest quiz grade is dropped!!
CS1 is usually a hard class however, with this proffesor it was pretty easy and she also prepares you for the FE.

A wonderful professor who care's so much about her students. Clear criteria's for what we need to learn, assignments are well defined, and she offers a lot of material for her students to pass in such a hard class. ABSOLUTELY take her for CS1!
Absolutely amazing professor, she did her best to equip students for success in a difficult class. 100% would take again.

She has her occasional hiccups when it comes to teaching lectures, but she really does care for her students; she provides a lot of studying material for exams, exams usually just go over the rudimentary theory of topics, she'll curve scores if class average is low, and she'll frequently extend due dates. She genuinely wants her students to pass.
My favorite professor so far at UCF. She was extremely kind and made sure to explain thoroughly. She was available to review during office hours. Would definitely reccomend!

One of the best professors I've had at UCF. She is amazing, understanding, caring. She really focuses on her students understanding the material. She is organized, and was always helpful in her office hours. The class is hard but she makes it easy by breaking down hard topic, she really prepares you. Couldn't ask anything more of a professor. 10/10
He is competent and understands the material. Tests are not too difficult. He doesn't grade homework quickly at all but other than that I have no complaints.

i had him for intro to discrete and didn't recognize his name when choosing classes and regretted it. he is a tough grader and blames students when the entire class average on an exam is low. only good part is he records lectures

I think he is a good professor, I really do. He is good at explaining topics, especially the videos uploaded after lectures. This class is just brutally difficult. The concepts are so complex and the exams are very challenging. I would take it with him, and the TA's are really helpful, but prepare to spend countless hours studying.

Dr. Rahaman is a great professor for cs2. His lectures are great even though i was not enrolled in his section. I would attend his lectures when i couldn't go to mine. Explains topics well and i was able to code up the examples on my own after his explanations

Dr. Rahaman did a great job explaining complex topics and concepts in an easily digestible manner. He is encouraging and caring. There are some extensive homework assignments that take a lot of time. He puts in the effort to prepare you for test material, but it is on you to truly practice material and put in the time outside of class.

This is for COP 3502H. CS1 is tough class in general and as an IT major really sucked. Regardless, Professor Rahaman was very kind, gave extra credit, and quizzes were manageable. Midterm and final were okay, but prog assignments helped somewhat. He is good for CS, IT, and other majors, but CS1 is just tough in general regardless of professor.

Rahaman is nice, but his lectures feel dry and boring to follow. He admits he's more used to teaching C than Java, which shows since it feels like a weaker version of Steinberg's material. Tests and assignments are easy except for a DP assignment. Disorganized, late test case updates, and very delayed grading. Easy class, but not much learning.

Terrifying Class. Was his first year of teaching so the final bits were rushed. CS1 is a HARD class, no matter who is teaching. He is very skilled in the subject and is learning how to convey that to others. Class was HEAVILY curved with him and was the only class that didn't have a mandatory "you need this % on final or you fail automatically".
Best professor for CS1 if you are a non CS major. Very easy course, with lots of extra credit. Most of the lecture he just read off PowerPoint slides, majority of my learning was done on my own time.

Lectures consist of him reading others' slides word for word and you can tell he doesn't read them before teaching them. A big chunk of the lab assignments had incorrect answers which students had to correct. Exams are done online, only final answers matter, not your work. Final exam average was 44%, he curved 26% back. Get ready to teach yourself.

Absolutely the best professor that teaches CS1. This was his first year teaching it and he did a great job. Gave us so many opportunities for extra credit and gave really great curves for both the midterm and final. Lectures were recorded so you can watch them on your on time. Labs did not require attendance and some quizzes were online. GOAT

Really nice professor, the material is hard but if you do the exercise pdfs and pay attention in lectures you should be fine. Attendance was not mandatory and the lectures were recorded, plenty of material was given to study and he would always stay after class to answer students' questions. Would take again, he's a kind person.

Be prepared to use tutoring because the lectures and slides are NOT enough. No required textbook, so definitely keep SARC / YouTube on hand for extra help. Typing out the homework was pretty annoying. He did give us a curve at the end, which was generous. He's a kind professor and very fair for such a rigorous course and never hesitated to help.
Overall a solid professor. He was able to explain topics well and was fair with exam material.
The course content was difficult for me but the professor was always easy to reach and a pleasure to speak with. Exams were weighted very high but he was very generous with extra credit and curves.

The course is so hard that the average for the final was a 44%. The homework is typed only, which, for a math course, sucked, so r the exams. Everything is lockdown browser except Homework and Labs. No textbook, all you have are his slides, and they suck. He threatened to have quizzes during class to force people to come; this never happened tho.

He was good at explaining the materials. We had the online recordings of the lectures (zoom) which helped me a lot. The tests were fair; if you go through the PowerPoint and do a few examples you will be fine. He was very understanding and cared about the students.
Very good professor!! Very fair with grading. His lectures are amazing and worth going to his office hours too!!
Quizzes are extremely difficult for no reason.

I took his CS1 class as a CS minor, Mechanical Engineering major. Guha does a great job at making you think hard in every problem, doesn't make anything easy, but doesn't throw any major curveballs either. Use his online notes and office hours and really make an effort to do the programming assignments, and he'll be the best teacher you can have.

His lectures weren't too bad but you have to understand how coding in the language works to stay up with him. His grading system is up to his discretion based on the averages of his students.. His test are graded hard as evidence by the grade you can have a low grade and still pass. He also talks fluently and very understandable.
Pointless lectures and made everything difficult for no reason. Bad at teaching.
#Phenomenal!
Great lecturer

Guha often gives test questions that aren't really covered in class. Tests are hard to predict, despite seeing examples. Homework is graded tough. He disrespected me a few times. One time I asked a question, only to be told to look it up in a dictionary. I reached out after the course for something, only to be answered initially, and the ignored.

Consisted of non-mandatory(you will fail if you dont attend) lectures w/ a quiz or assignment at end of week. Assignments are straightforward , grading was fairly lenient with a curve being applied throughout the course. Expect quizzes to be highly difficult w/ averages often sitting at a C, despite this with curving these often turned into B's.

The class was really hard and the exams were brutal, but I do think he really cares about students and wants you to really learn C deeply. He gave out a lot of extra credit and you should definitely do all of it. He also curves like crazy. I wouldn't recommend taking him if you aren't a CS major, though.

Great professor! Though exams were tough (with the class average usually being around 50%), assignments and lab grades helped boost my grade. The professor does offer extra credit opportunities so pay attention. He does not like to repeat himself about it. Make some friends and don't procrastinate. An A is very doable. You will learn a lot.

Gives little extra credit on assignments and exams. Study early for exams at least 2 weeks. Take his advice of studying 12+ hours and writing 3 practice programs weekly. If you're stuck on a concept, utilize his office hours as he explains them well. Be sure to understand programming assignments. Ask good questions in lecture. Helpful LA sessions.

The course content is not bad. This professor will teach you 3 things about each 10 subjects and then test you on seven of those topics but will not even hint as to what he will test on (aka you must know everything). Doesn't really do partial credit. KEEP YOUR QUIZZES (extra credit)! Will be helpful to create study group.

Good professor, but "Cryptography" was probably the hardest class I've taken at UCF. I understand that he was trying to challenge us, but sometimes it just felt unnecessary, especially to anyone who has a life or a job. He also likes to throw curveball questions on quizzes/tests. But hey, he curved the class, and I got a B, so I can't complain.

Pros: His lectures are decent. He does a good job makes the lectures engaging and interesting.

Cons: Grading scale is weird. He decides it at the end based on the class average. Tests are often more difficult than needed. Average on the final was 46. He often throws “curveballs” into his tests. Half of the final had these unexpected questions.

This guy absolutely sucks. Why does he have 100+, 5 rating? This guy makes an elective harder than CS1. The TAs in this class were horrendous, especially this Ian Binstock kid. Saw the kid finish grading each exam in like 10 seconds.

For someone who emphasizes the attendance being non-mandatory, he sure does incentivize showing up. Arbitrarily gave everyone that came in to pick up their quizzes extra points after he realized the average for his class was a 41.

I passed his class but he sure didn't let it easy on the masses. Class discord complained about him nonstop… Good luck

This guy is good at teaching, but the course material in COT3100 is hard. DON'T skip classes just because there's notes online, you will not understand it nearly half as well as if you just went to the lecture and paid attention. People will complain about the class being hard and those are the guys who didn't try to learn at all.

I think his style of teaching doesn't really fit a begginer course like Intro to C. He's caring but he will give you a tough time. If you're a beginner in coding, I would suggest a different professor but if you're very studious and look for a challenge, you can take this class.
He's a non-factor, and I refuse to waste another breath on him. Middle finger all the way!

Professor Guhas grading is horrendous. If he suspects you of using ai for anything at all, even if you clearly didn't and you plead your case you will get a zero on that assignment. If you write out a 3-4 paragraph email explaining how and why your work was done by you he will just say he doesn't believe you and leave it at that.
Strict grader & non-reasonable
Terrible system. Take someone else.

He's cool when it comes to gradings and homeworks. His grading system is a little weird (he decides what's considered an A, B, etc at the end of the semester), but its beneficial. Main problem: quizzes and tests. He writes them the DAY before and they make little to no sense. Its like trying to decipher Shakespeare and then code. Its ridiculous.

Best Professor of my 4 years of studying for mechanical engineering. I took this class as an elective and it was insanely hard but I learned more than I have in any of my classes by far. I am considering systems simulations masters due to how well he taught this course and how much I learned.

One of my favorite classes! Great for practicing coding and algorithms. Can be a bit challenging given the time constraints of the weekly "quizzes" (i.e. competitions), but you can get 80% partial credit over the weekend for any missed questions, which is pretty generous. Heavily curved, and passing is virtually guaranteed if you put in the effort.
Guha is the GPA reaper and his scythe is discrete math.

Difficult class. HW is time consuming and exams are hard. Quizzes are fair. Required much self study and DEEP comprehension. Must take advantage of all the past materials posted to get a good feel of what questions will be asked (although different every time). Guha has good lectures but it can be hard to follow along. Look ahead on the content.

Definitely a good person and a knowledgeable teacher that has a passion for it. However, incredibly advanced work asked of you. Homework is harder than the lecture, and tests are even harder than the homework. For perspective: a C is currently set at a 45%.
Tough. Engaging beyond 110%. Strict but fair professor. Not for the light-hearted. Peak university level class.

The course grade is exam heavy and he puts hard things he never even taught or mentioned in the class on his exams and homework just because he thinks they are "fun" or "cool" problems. I got my AA degree and had a 4.0 gpa, so I know what I'm doing. But now there goes me gpa. Thanks Guha for messing that up for me. Do not take if you like your gpa.

Amazing professor who has passion about what he's teaching. Resources are all available online, including past exams. His lectures do move quickly, but given the amount of topics to cover, the pace is fair. Assignments are good and engaging, but most weight goes to tests. Tests require a lot of creativity & understanding of topics. You will learn!

He is an amazing teacher and good grades will be achieved as long as you pay attention in class and attend lectures. Though there are many tests and lectures, he really makes sure that you understand the topic and he gives good feedback. He is also easily accessible if help is needed.

I'm still in this class, but from the first couple of weeks I can already tell this isn't going well The grade is extremely test-heavy Lecture attendance isn't required, but if you miss one day you better spend an entire day reviewing notes or watching youtube Recitation is required attendance but it doesn't even cover the same material as in class

I recommend Professor Guha to high-level students. He is very knowledgeable, so he covers content quickly. This makes him more difficult, but it can be worthwhile because students will learn more. There are two types of exam problems: some are straightforward, and others require intuition and a creative step.

Guha is an excellent communicator and super smart guy. However, his lectures move quickly and can give the impression that students are simply expected to know what he's explaining already. Not an easy class by any means. Tough homework & exams. Two programming projects. Labs are mandatory & graded. Guha is a great person to talk to outside class.

The man the myth, Took him for CS2 & Discrete. I would say the best thing about Guha is his webpage, he has previous tests & notes there etc. His tests & assignments are notoriously hard but you will learn from them. Lectures are ok, I didn't get much out of them. You will have to take his test for the FE so he is inevitable.
whoa. my worst semester ever, this was absolutely insane. almost lost my mind

Guha is one of the best CS professors you can have. It's clear he has an immense passion for math, which makes his lectures very engaging. His TA's office hours are very helpful and gives credit for them. He assigns one homework per week (not hard if you go to lectures). His exams are not that hard, just go over previous exams and you'll be fine.

Dr. Guha was an alright lecturer. It was hard to pay attention because the lectures are boring and his way of taking notes is quite terrible (he just writes on paper and uploads it online in a non-visual way). Be prepared that his class is very lecture heavy and his TAs are VERY laid back in grading and knowing the concepts. However, Guha is caring

This class was unnecessarily difficult. Would cover topics and examples too fast, and not explain much. Looking back at his old coursework and tests was the best thing for this class, for homework and tests. Be prepared to spend hours trying to teach yourself things you should have learned in class.

You can tell how much Guha loves math / CS, and he really tries to relay that to his students. I think he's a great professor overall - very intelligent, clear course outline, great at teaching. Only downside is there's a ton of content to cover, so his lectures are VERY fast-paced, but he'll almost always stop and answer questions that arise.

He's a teacher who cares about the content he is teaching, which means he makes it difficult on purpose. Little homework. For some reason, he has projects that involves code and grades it based off that. If you like the subject, take him, if you don't care, don't bother. He's not worth the effort if you don't like this type of math.

Self studying is your best friend, his lectures aren't good. the notes he writes in class are messy and frustrating to follow along with. if you aren't going to attend class, be there to pick up exams bc he might do surprise extra credit. pretty difficult class overall. avoid unless you are doing SCAN minor.

DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR IF YOU CARE ABOUT YOUR GPA. Tests and homework are very difficult. Includes random "cool" things on tests that he briefly mentions in class, with no indication that that material will be on the test. "Hints" to things that might be on the test that he's not going to teach and expects you to teach yourself.
You'll have to teach yourself everything. The only way to know what's going to be on the test is by finding his previous exams. Rarely responds to emails. Take anyone else for discrete.
At least the good thing about this class is that you learn to enjoy the simple things in life.

Very clear on policies and resources. Occasionally references a textbook but the textbook is purely supplementary and online. He gives us access to all his past class resources: quizzes/homeworks/lectures/notes and even the class grade bands and grade spread. Hard material, but will answer all questions thoroughly. Lenient with certain deadlines.

He goes through the material very quickly and expects you to already understand the topics. Gets irritated when you ask questions because in his mind you should understand everything. You will be teaching yourself the material, notes barely help. Letter grade is based on the standard deviation of the class. The average exam grade is a 45-50%

Expects that you already know the material and doesn't go into depth with some topics. The labs / TAs aren't a big help either. You have to teach yourself or go to the SI or SARC sessions. He teaches pretty fast, so if you don't understand the material to start, take another professor.

The legendary Guha. He has lived up to his reputation. I did not enjoy my time in this class. Content was difficult and the pace was too fast. Recitations were not helpful as TAs just give you problems to solve instead of teaching you. Do not take this class with this professor. Great guy, not teacher.
Made my life a living hell. DO NOT TAKE HIM!!!!

I'm sure Guha is a great professor for other classes but he is very unforgiving, snarky to his students, and he expects students to already intuitively understand the material. Lectures feel more like a showcase of his knowledge than actually learning the material, and his notes are often not helpful. I'd recommend a different professor.

Guha teaches very fast and often assumes that everybody understands the material. Is condescending anytime someone asks a question about something they don't quite understand. Don't expect any help (or sympathy). Be ready to self study! Anyway try to get another prof, but if you can't its not the end of the world.

Had Guha back in the Fall 2023 semester and just didn't get around to rating him till now. I learned a lot in his class and I was super prepared for the FE. However the process of doing so was stressful, way more stressful and unnecessarily competitive had I taken a different professor. Many friends got Bs that could've been As with other profs.

Great professor, super smart. If you want to make sure you learn the material and pass the Foundations Exam, take him. He gives very detailed lectures and is open to answering your questions. Don't take his class with other hard classes. Only issue I had was with the TA. He decides grade cutoffs at the end of the semester but they are quite fair.

This man has a MASSIVE ego and this was seen in his office hours. This class made me question if I chose the right major (computer engineering) and I do not recommend this guy to anyone. If you want to learn the same material in a more fair environment, take someone else. Enough said.

They all say, "if you want to learn, take Guha regardless of the grade you get". At the end of the day, grad school admissions and employers see the grade you got in the class - not how much u learned in class. It was horrible not knowing what your letter grade was and everyone praying on their classmates' downfall due to this man's grading system.
Great professor overall: if you want to learn take this class, enough said.

Builds on skills from COP 3503. Homework is much easier than COP 3502, however, tests seem much more difficult. As long as you cover material from class you should easily be able to get a B. Attend lecture as he might hint what things will be on the exam.

Guha was decent at lecturing for discrete, however still unnecessarily pushes people's limits. The TA, Thomas Kern, was the absolute worst. He would often mark right HW answers wrong (can't be corrected due to Guha's syllabus policy), and was very sarcastic and passive-aggressive both during virtual office hours and Webcourses submission comments.

If you value your mental wellbeing and your GPA (especially if you're taking other hard classes), steer clear of Guha for CS1! Guha pushes peoples limits in this course. I'm not a fan of his teaching style or grading philosophy (he decides A/B/C cutoffs at the end of the term). The same CS1/FE goals can be accomplished with a different professor!

If you care about learning and want to pass the FE no matter what, take Professor Guha. He will make sure you finish the class having a clear understanding of every single topic. However, his assignments and exams are incredibly difficult. If you do not care about your GPA, this is a great professor, otherwise take another professor.

Programming assignments took everything out of me. Do not take this course with other difficult courses. Loves putting information you could only get from attending lectures on the exams. Very smart man, but moves very quickly through material if he thinks a topic is easy. I found it easier than CS1 but many of my classmates disagree.

Where to start..Professor Guha is an amazing professor but definitely not for everyone. He is not an easy A but with enough effort you will surely pass the class and learn a lot. He pushes his students to the max because he knows CS major is hard and want to prepare them for the outside world. He will give you all the tools to do good in his class.

Guha is an amazing beacon of not watering down CS concepts in education. CS is hard and he refuses to hide it from anyone. He is very considerate about grading (as he grades on a a bell curve) and he has resources on his website, so given enough drive anyone can succeed in his class. It is tough but very manageable (my Senior) year.

Unnecessarily hard for no reason. He always tries to sneak in some competitive programming tricks into the assignment and quiz. For example, why on earth would anyone force students to do bitwise when students are studying BFS and DFS?

Most of the class was based on 5 quizzes and 1 final. Homework is quite difficult; a few questions will be easy math and the rest will be code-breaking. If you are into Cybersecurity, this class will be interesting. Show up to class as you might get hints on the homework. Learn Java & Python as these will make doing the code-breaking a lot easier.

CS1 is a difficult class. I feel like I have learned a lot from Guha. Yes, his homework and tests are difficult; but if you put in the time and effort you can do well. Attendance isn't mandatory, but I highly encourage you to show up as you can gain lots of info from him. Take him if you are CS. He will help you pass the FE!

I have taken Ahmed, Meade and now Guha and he is probably the worst professor to possibly take. Go take Steinberg for CS2 if you want to pass. This man is praying on every one of his students downfall.

The topics themselves were easy to grasp but I felt like Professor Guha made it harder than it needed to be on the quizzes and the homework. Attendance isn't mandatory, but I would suggest going to lectures because it's kinda hard to go through the material yourself. He curved the grades at the end, but just make sure you're understanding.

He was a nice guy and you could tell he cared, but he moved fast. If you didn't already know certain concepts, it wasn't easy to keep up. The homework/ programming assignments always sucked the life out of me, and the exams were always difficult. Lectures are required but if you don't attend them you will not pass.

I studied DSA online at Princeton and MIT before taking this course (for LC purposes). And Guha has a very similar teaching style to the top professors at the top school. He's so passionate and knowledgeable. It is tough, but it is the way it should be. I'm taking CS2 with him.
His class was the only one that I felt my money was well spent on.
Tough class but i enjoyed it. Guha is a good professor

I honestly don't know how his rating is so high. Yes, this class is normally difficult, but Guha was one of the most rude and unhelpful professors I've ever had. Super patronizing at his office hours (attend the TAs instead) and forget about him responding to emails. This is the only class I've ever gotten lower than an A in. Take a different prof.

I really enjoyed this class with Professor Guha. It's definitely not an Easy A but it's not hard to pass either. If you put enough time and effort, you will surely pass easily. He is super well organized, he puts everyday notes in his website and is easy to find. Study previous quizzes from his previous semesters, they are all in his website!

Out of all the possible CS1 professors, take Guha. He is an amazing professor who cares about his students. Also, he is the one who writes the foundation exam so you will be the best prepared out of everyone else because his exams/tests are basically foundation exam questions. Hard class but easiest with Guha.

Professor Guha is a great teacher, but the class can be quite intimidating. He wants everyone who takes the class to be challenged so assignments and tests can be difficult. However, he curves the grade so that 50% to 45% is usually a passing grade. I would highly recommend hit to anyone who truly wants to learn the material.

DSA was never an easy course, any good school will make your life a living hell taking this class, not just UCF. Guha was a great professor, very knowledgeable about what he teaches. I have no complaints. 
I know it isn't easy, but it is what it is, CS was never intended to be an easy major. No pain no gain.
Professor Guha is an awesome professor and I would definitely recommend him. He is awesome! He can be tough at times but he is still amazing
Mr. Guha was an awesome teacher this year! His work isn't to hard or easy. He is super inspirational and I would definitely recommend him for others interested in taking CS1. Keep it going Arup!

Dr. Guha is a great professor for those who actually want to learn. I recommend going to his office hours and attending all lectures. Do the homework and review all the resources provided and you will pass.

On his website he has all of his previous class material posted, take advantage of this. Looking at his past assignments is the best way to do the homework and study for the tests! He is a vary lenient grader, there is a massive curve placed on the final grading scale. Just do the homework and you will be fine.

Arup is pretty good. The class is very tough and requires creative thinking to solve problems. Honors section has an additional final project which is time-consuming but not too bad. Tests are pretty tough, but there's a good curve at the end of the semester. Overall, he's a good guy and he's very accessible to provide help outside class.
Class is hard but easy to pass. Teachers like this are generally what make your students become better. Highly recommend if you actually want to learn.

His homeworks are very difficult and really get you thinking, he has a lot of notes up on his website and publishes the in class notes he writes. His lectures are not mandatory but you would not pass class without attending them.

He forms mandatory study groups. Meet 1 hr/week. Honors has a final project with little guidance/grading criteria. He's intentionally vague because he grades "relative to your ability" or something. Luckily, Honors gets the huge curve from the standard class. Tip: Learn LaTeX asap! You're in CS. Google is your friend. Search "LaTeX tutorial"

Overall, Guha is a great professor and genuinely wants his students to learn. His lectures often go fast, and his homework assignments and exams are very challenging, but he posts a ton of resources on his website to help you, and he is always very helpful in office hours. If you're up for a challenge and willing to put in the work, take him.

It's easy to pass this class (~50% = C), but getting a B / A requires lots of time & effort. The ten HW is worth 1/5th of the overall grade so, getting a 90%+ average will carry your grade. His lecture style is fast. Depending on your math affinity, TA help might be needed. Many questions are prior math competition ones or around that level.

He is a good teacher and the stuff he teaches is really interesting! However, his homework assignments and quizzes are far beyond what he teaches in class. Be ready to teach yourself a lot. He does a good job providing previous quizzes/hw assignments with answers and grading. His extra credit is practically a punishment for students who have a job.

This class information wise - has been one of my favorites. However, in terms of coursework it has been incredibly demoralizing and difficult. He intentionally makes questions impossibly difficult. He states as much. He has said - "I don't expect any of you to solve this." And then proceed to make that question worth 50% of the homework.

Lectures are really interesting and you will learn a lot but the Homework is extremely hard. Most of the course is breaking encrypted code and it can be very frustrating at times. Tests are surprisingly way easier than homework assignments so you will probably get a better grade on tests. Guha curves the grades so that a ~50% is a C.

Very good professor that genuinely wants his students to learn. The homework assignments and tests are very hard but he gives many resources that help out a lot. Curves the grades at the end of the year so that a C is around a 50% so it's not hard to pass but he will make you work for a B or an A. You will learn a lot but will have to study a lot.
He is ok at best. My main problem with him is that he never responds to emails.

Guha teaches very engaging lectures, and posts many notes and example programs for studying. However, the assignments/quizzes are challenging and have hard deadlines, so spend LOTS of time on them and stay on your toes. His class may also be too fast and less forgiving for newer CS students, so if you're confident, take him and you'll learn a lot.

Problem solving class is just basically leetcode questions. Class is hard but he curves appropriately so as long as you're around the mean for the class you'll do fine. Definitely one of the best teachers in the CS department since he makes you actually do work.

Honestly, not a great professor. I would not believe the good reviews either to be honest... He explains way too fast for material that is fundamental to CS students. I'd go with another professor who is better and doesn't speed through the material. Your understanding of the fundamentals will thank you for that.

Guha is the best prof I've had in the CS program thus far. Yes the class is difficult, but so is CS in general. If you find yourself struggling on Assignment 0 then it means you need to brush up on your Intro to C stuff. The people complaining in other comments were never at office hours. Guha is always ready to help.

He's like a harsh father who expects too much of you. Guha is an extremely caring professor, however his tests and homework make him seem like he doesnt. All of the work is pretty difficult. Not impossible at all though, if you take this and any other class with him extremely seriously from the get go, you will do great. You really learn too.

I just recently took this professor for Comp Sci 1. These recent positive reviews have to be bots. There is no way in heck this guy could get such positive reception after such a disaster of a semester. Beware.

This class was very challenging. Professor guha is definitely one of the better CS professors at UCF so take him! There were four quizzes and a final exam. The quizzes were challenging but doable if you put in the effort to study and pay attention in lectures. We also had mandatory study groups that were 10% of our final grade for the course.

Let me start by saying that I love CS. But Guha really did his best to make me not like it for this semester. Crazy hard assignments that only about 15% of the class did good in. Quizzes are super hard and not doable at all. No drops or re-do's, but you do get a huge curve at the end. Lectures are fast and don't prepare you for tests. Skip him.

Barely explains concepts. If you don't get an outside tutor, you'll fail. Extremely difficult quizzes, and the quizzes come before projects, so you don't have experience programming with the concepts you're quizzed on. Last project was something he admitted most wouldn't be able to finish. Makes COP3502 much more difficult than it needs to be.

The professor shows a complete lack of empathy & uses his "years of experience" with students to validate a completely unfair work schedule (Making work due on Holidays, and little-no class time actually covering needed subjects for big assignments) NEEDED an expensive tutor to pass. Often rudely laughed at questions, terrible at teaching basics.

Dr. Guha is the best CS professor at UCF in my opinion, this class is very similar to his other classes. There's only homework, quizzes, and a final exam. Don't slack on the homework as the grade can really catch up to you. Study immensely for the quizzes, and don't fret about a low grade he always changes his grading scale!

Not bad, (I think this class becomes COP 2500 in Fall 2021) Great for an intro to CS, and teaching Python. I think Arup was nicer because it is an example of that even experts can learn, as he learned of universities using python at a convention a few years ago (he says he doesn't go to many). This was taken Spring 2019 into COVID-19.

Mr. Guha is as talented/smart with CS as much as he is medicore/terrible with regards to teaching. The class is pretty much DIY and he is kind of passive aggressive especially if you don't understand his inscrutable lectures. Unless you have experience in the field of CS, please avoid this individual.

Worst UCF prof, almost universal opinion. Passive-ag, rude, God complex, does not value or respect students. Says he "provides scaffolding" to excuse his poor, incomplete lectures. Falsely accused SEVERAL kids of cheating on an exam and deducted points. Very brief (if any) responses to long/detailed emails. Extremely difficult, provides no support.

The dude is extremely smart but the class was very hard. He is very good at explaining things and you can tell he is one of the smartest CS professor at the school. The material is really difficult towards the end because it gets very theoretical. The programming HW is near impossible to get a 100 on but everything else is doable. Very caring guy.

Difficult class in general with lots of assignments and homework. Mainly everything is about your process as he gives lots of partial credit. This class does take a lot of time since almost all of it is creative thinking. Does go fast through lectures so you do have to study on your own. Overall would take again if theres no one easier.
His classes are enjoyable, but his exams are really hard compared to the basic stuff he shows in class. Expect to learn on your own.

As a person, Guha is nice and respectful. However, he flies through the material (which isn't easy) since he knows it well but that causes students to be lost. Gives work to raise your grade, but you'll end up having to learn everything on your own since he flies through the material as previously mentioned. His class is equivalent to 2-3 classes.

A good intro course for Python and he is good at explaining the basics. The lectures were long and somewhat fast however they were recorded over zoom and saved to go back and watch later. He's a very nice guy but the homework given each week was a coding project and towards the end even with watching the lectures were different than what's taught .

Lots of self learning, tough grader. Puts questions on tests that he knows no one will be able to solve. Lectures barely cover anything you need for tests/quizzes. Very strict and won't help you during the tests if needed. Didn't care if people submitted quizzes late during COVID, gave them 0's anyways. Locked old test/quiz so we couldn't studythem
Long boring lectures and comes up with random projects that are very hard.

Great teacher! He truly does care and knows what he's talking about. That being said... the class is just plain hard. You have to understand problem-solving in order to pass this class. If you just write the correct answer you get a 1/8, but a wrong answer with logically reasoning gets you at 7/8. A massive curve at the end by 2 letter grades too.

Teacher has huge personal interest in the subject, and expects that students will be equally enthusiastic. Prepare for lots of self-learning since lectures will often tangent and not pertain to quizzes and tests. Aims for low grades numerically and curves the class afterwards, puts questions he expects you to fail in homework and exam.

I really like Guha. I also had him for intro to discrete, and as long as you either pay attention in class or go through his notes (which are all posted online) and put effort into the homework you'll do well on the quizzes or tests, there's never any surprises on them. He actually enjoys the subjects he teaches, and it shows.

Tough professor, makes sure you get the grade you earn. His grading scale fluctuates every semester, so at times you feel a bit blind with grades. That said, the content of the course is very interesting and engaging. If you enjoy breaking codes and problem solving, take this class. HW questions don't always line up with what we're quizzed on.

Professor Guha is an excellent source of information, however he cannot teach at all. The methods he uses to convey information to students in my opinion is counter-intuitive to learning. If you ever have him I would rely on his SI Sessions/ Office Hours which help immensely. Goodluck.

Discrete is definitely a hard course, but Guha is a great professor for it. He may not give you the answer you want, but he will definitely teach you all you need to know about the class as well as a lot about yourself. I really recommend him as a professor.

Easily the most fun and unique class I've taken at UCF. Guha is a great professor and extremely knowledgeable about the subject. The bulk of the class is essentially cracking codes either by hand, or by writing tools to assist you (brute-force). However, if you don't put in effort to actually learn the material, you will do poorly.

He expects you to work hard, but he gives you all the tools you need to succeed. His website has tons of material from past semesters, study it thoroughly and you'll be ready for whatever he throws at you on the test.

The weekly program assignments have an average of 50% and the first exam did as well. In the lectures, he just writes code at lightning speed with no explanation. You have to figure out everything about C. He repeatedly emails us saying, it is our fault if we are struggling. Just because his class is hard doesn't mean you'll learn a lot. Take Szum.

Practice!!! Really tough, high expectations. I passed with an A without the curve so its possible. His lectures are great and you can easily contact him. He links to all his past course material which you need to use for practice. I had a part time job and 3 other classes, so if you want to excel you will need to 10+ hrs dedicated for this class

Listen, he is a great guy! I really do like him as a person. However the level of effort I put into this class was outrageous. He is a terrible teacher. Let's be honest nobody truly cares about the theory. We are taking this class because UCF requires it to earn the degree. Please cut the BS and stop looking for every possible way to make it hard!

Arup is a cool guy. He went to MIT so he knows what it means to be competitively taught, thus he also wants to pass on that type of teaching. He will challenge everyone in the class, but he is still fair with grading when it comes to it. During the covid19 crisis, he was also effective in dealing with cheaters / online classes.

One of the best professors I had so far. Complex topics are explained very well and with relatable explanations. Professor has a good understanding of what he teaches. Tries to prepare students for jobs, exams, etc. Gives challenging questions that make you think creatively. Shows creative problem solving in class. Interesting lectures.

Guha is passionate & has high expectations. Taking thorough notes & really understanding the material is key to getting an A, because he will ask theoretical questions every test. His previous tests are on his website, which are a good study tool for getting a B. He wouldn't budge on letting us use calculators even though the math was rough.

Prof. Guha really has this amazing energy and enthusiasm that he brings to lectures. Though this class was incredibly difficult, he gives a lot of partial credit and curves the class as a whole at the end of the semester. He has great resources from his past classes available to students and they are great to use to practice for quizzes and exams.

Very hard class, prof makes it harder than it should be. I still don't really understand what the class was about/what I actually learned from it. Arup is honestly a great guy, but seems to be out of touch with his students. I would take him as a professor again, just not for this class. Still, he does care about his students + being a good prof

People like to Bash on Arup for being too hard or whatever, but In my opinion if you put in more than minimal amounts of work for his class, youll do fine. Go to class, read his lecture notes on his website, and go over previous semesters exam questions to study and you will pass the class. The curve at the end of the class is very generous too.

He's good if you have an extremely strong mathematical foundation before entering his class. I mean REALLY strong, like MIT student level understanding. His class is really fast paced, and I believe most people won't be able to keep up. He also focuses on problems he finds interesting during class but they usually don't show up during exams.

Avoid Guha at all costs for this class. He makes the course much harder than it needs to be, and you will need to learn most of the material outside of class. The average GPA in my class was a 2.13 -- and that was after a significant portion of the class withdrew. It isn't hard to pass (a 48 was a C ), but the class isn't worth the stress.

Lectures are mostly useless and I had to teach myself most of the material. Teaches one thing in class, then tests on different material. Tests are "creative" and also feature random math questions outside of the course curriculum. Exam averages in the 40s and 50s, then curves grades at the end of the year. 117 students got a C-/C/C+ this semester.

3362. To pass you roughly need a 50. You need to show up to class everyday if you dont want to do poorly. Guha is notorious for giving you no time to take a test and making crazy hard questions. Make sure you submit assignments correctly or you will get a 0. No textbooks needed. Group HW. Cheat sheets on last few exams. With that, still very hard

Mr. Guha is a good professor. I feel like he gets a bad rep due to the difficulty of the class, but there's really no escaping from that; it's a VERY hard class.
